title: The Second Gift
caption: Harriet Bloom's archive yielded its surprises the way old houses yield drafts: quietly, and only to those patient enough to notice. While Chloe stood beside her with notebook ready, Harriet unfolded a yellowed document and found a line that seemed to have been waiting decades for the right pair of eyes: a mention of the founding family's 'second gift,' hidden beside the western steps until it was remembered. The phrase struck the room like a match, lighting both Harriet's scholarly suspicion and Chloe's immediate wonder. They spread an old map over the table and saw the impossible there in ink — another place, absent from the public record and somehow still intact in memory. Harriet, who trusted paper more than rumor, admitted that Willowmere might have been keeping one more secret after all. Chloe answered with the eagerness Harriet had already come to recognize: if the town had left a thread lying loose, they would follow it.
location: Harriet Bloom's basement archive, warm with lamplight, crowded with boxes, photographs, and an old neighborhood map spread across the worktable
story_notes: Harriet discovers a reference to a second founding-family location hidden in her archive, opening a new Willowmere mystery for Chloe to pursue.
